title: AI chat layout
subtitle: Pinned header, pinned composer, page scrollbar
draftPlaceholder: Ask AI chat something or start a new conversation

messages.author: assistant
messages.label: AI chat
messages.text: Maybe AI chat can handle your query. Or it can help you shape a follow-up before you send anything on.

messages.author: user
messages.text: Can you remind me what this example is showing?

messages.author: assistant
messages.label: AI chat
messages.text: This thread is only a layout demo. The header stays pinned to the top, the composer stays pinned to the bottom, and the browser owns the vertical scrollbar.

messages.author: assistant
messages.label: AI chat
messages.text: Because the scrollbar belongs to the page, the transcript can travel underneath the composer as you scroll back through older messages.

messages.author: user
messages.text: So the header controls do not move away with the transcript?

messages.author: assistant
messages.label: AI chat
messages.text: Exactly. New chat and History live in the header, and the layout mode keeps that header stuck to the top edge while the content keeps scrolling.

messages.author: assistant
messages.label: AI chat
messages.text: The composer also gets bottom padding applied to the transcript, so the latest message can settle above the composer instead of hiding behind it.

messages.author: user
messages.text: And when I scroll up, older messages can still pass behind the composer for that layered chat feel.

messages.author: assistant
messages.label: AI chat
messages.text: Right. The layout is doing the pinning. The example transcript is just static JSON so you can focus on whether the shell behaves correctly.

messages.author: assistant
messages.label: AI chat
messages.text: That also makes it easier to regression-test: no live model, no request timing, just a deterministic chat history exercising long-scroll behavior.

messages.author: user
messages.text: Good. I just want the underlying layout primitive to own this behavior cleanly.

messages.author: assistant
messages.label: AI chat
messages.text: That is the point of this example. If this layout feels correct here, the app layer can inherit it instead of overriding the content panel with one-off CSS.
